subject: SCIENCE
title: Material Detectives
objective: Classify familiar examples as solid, liquid, or gas using observable properties.
introduction: Solids keep a shape, liquids flow and take a container’s shape, and gases spread to fill available space; these are three common states, not the only states. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Material Detectives”.
A key word used in the lesson “Material Detectives”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Solids keep a shape, liquids flow and take a container’s shape, and gases spread to fill available space; these are three common states, not the only states.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Solids keep a shape, liquids flow and take a container’s shape, and gases spread to fill available space; these are three common states, not the only states.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

doesNotFit: Only three states can ever exist

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: Which familiar material is a liquid?

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. stone and air do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

options.text: Solids keep a shape, liquids flow and take a container’s shape, and gases spread to fill available space; these are three common states, not the only states.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Solids keep a shape, liquids flow and take a container’s shape, and gases spread to fill available space; these are three common states, not the only states.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Solids keep a shape, liquids flow and take a container’s shape, and gases spread to fill available space; these are three common states, not the only states.

The correct response is “stone—solid” because it follows the lesson explanation: Solids keep a shape, liquids flow and take a container’s shape, and gases spread to fill available space; these are three common states, not the only states.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “Smoke is simply a gas” because it follows the lesson explanation: Solids keep a shape, liquids flow and take a container’s shape, and gases spread to fill available space; these are three common states, not the only states.

items.prompt: Which familiar material is a liquid?
items.explanation: The correct response is “water” because it follows the lesson explanation: Solids keep a shape, liquids flow and take a container’s shape, and gases spread to fill available space; these are three common states, not the only states.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?

options.text: Only three states can ever exist

items.explanation: The correct response is “air—gas” because it follows the lesson explanation: Solids keep a shape, liquids flow and take a container’s shape, and gases spread to fill available space; these are three common states, not the only states.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Material Detectives; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Material Detectives.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
